subject: Persistent volumes for Database storage
commit_message:
Persistent volumes for Database storage

Addition of persistent volumes for all DBs including
persistence of policy mariadb (with initial configuration data)

Change-Id: I029e724c3f8bba7fe3599b0955a4a3d213c08163
Issue-ID: OOM-268
Signed-off-by: BorislavG <Borislav.Glozman@amdocs.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Hi Borislav, mysql should be able to create those files upon container creation. We should avoid this as I see this as forking. you have added 109mb worth of files

Patch Set 1:

Borislav, looking at it now

Patch Set 1:

> Hi Borislav, mysql should be able to create those files upon
 > container creation. We should avoid this as I see this as forking.
 > you have added 109mb worth of files

Hi Munir,
I agree with you, however policy db container has pre-configured DB, stored in the docker image. If we mount the directory for it, we lose the data and the container won't start. 
We can skip persisting this container, but then we can lose the data.
So this is necessary if we chose to persist policy db.

Patch Set 1:

> > Hi Borislav, mysql should be able to create those files upon
 > > container creation. We should avoid this as I see this as
 > forking.
 > > you have added 109mb worth of files
 > 
 > Hi Munir,
 > I agree with you, however policy db container has pre-configured
 > DB, stored in the docker image. If we mount the directory for it,
 > we lose the data and the container won't start.
 > We can skip persisting this container, but then we can lose the
 > data.
 > So this is necessary if we chose to persist policy db.

But do you need preload them? mysql should be able to put those from the docker image itself.

We have persisted mariadb data before and without having to copy the data prior to container startup.

Patch Set 1:

> > > Hi Borislav, mysql should be able to create those files upon

 > But do you need preload them? mysql should be able to put those
 > from the docker image itself.
 > 
 > We have persisted mariadb data before and without having to copy
 > the data prior to container startup.

Please suggest how to persist this specific container. I looked at the other mariadb containers and same approach doesn't work. The problem is that this container is pre-loaded with DB schema etc. It is like a snapshot of a DB.

Otherwise, is this commit OK?

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

I am good with YAML files but we need to deal with the database files so they are not part of config.

I will open a defect on portal to provide deployment without pre-configured DB.
